basic_details.job_title: ITAT Private Secretary Recruitment 2026: 42 Posts, Apply Online
basic_details.organization_name: Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT), Ministry of Law & Justice, Government of India
basic_details.job_location: Various Benches of ITAT across India
basic_details.short_information: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT), Ministry of Law & Justice, Government of India, has issued Vacancy Circular No. F.301-Ad(ATD)/DR/2026-27 inviting online applications from Indian nationals for filling up existing and anticipated vacancies in the cadres of Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary (both Group B Gazetted) at various Benches of ITAT across the country. A total of 42 posts are notified - 22 Senior Private Secretary and 20 Private Secretary. Selection is through a Written Examination, Skill Test and Personal Interview. This article covers the ITAT Private Secretary Re

important_dates.last_date_to_apply: Within 30 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in "Employment News"

qualification.details: Applications will be rejected if they are: | Incomplete Not supported by duly self-attested copies of relevant documents in support of age and educational qualifications Not accompanied by relevant certificates issued by the Competent Authority regarding caste, non-creamy layer status, disability or EWS category, wherever applicable Submitted by applicants against whom any criminal case is pending | The candidature of any candidate found resorting to malpractice or canvassing will be outrightly rejected.

salary_details.senior private secretary: ₹47,600 to ₹1,51,100 - Pay Level-8 (Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC)
salary_details.private secretary: ₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400 - Pay Level-7 (Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC)

selection_process: Paper 1 General English 100 Essay of minimum 250 words, Letter Writing, Precis Writing and Grammar
Paper 2 General Knowledge & Reasoning 100 General Knowledge/General Studies, Logical Reasoning, Current Affairs
Skill Test 100 English Shorthand @120 WPM, English Typing @45 WPM
Category Minimum Qualifying Marks in Each Paper Aggregate Marks of Both Papers
Recruitment is through a Written Examination
The written examination and skill test will be conducted at 9 stations: Delhi
Ahmedabad and Hyderabad. Candidates must opt for two centres of choice in order of preference. If a sufficient number of candidates is not received for a particular station
ITAT has the sole discretion to cancel that centre
and the candidate will be required to appear at the second centre chosen by them.
The Personal Interview carries 75 marks. Only candidates shortlisted after the Skill Test will be called for the Personal Interview; the date and venue will be notified later.

The Skill Test will be evaluated only for candidates who secure the minimum qualifying marks in the Written Examination. The overall merit list will be drawn on the basis of combined merit acquired in the Skill Test and the Personal Interview. Both the Written Examination papers and the Skill Test will be conducted in English medium only.
SC/ST candidates already in service of the Central/State Government
shall be reimbursed 2nd Class Ordinary/Sleeper Class railway fare or bus fare
on production of tickets as per Government of India guidelines
for appearing in the Skill Test and Personal Interview.
how_to_apply: Visit the ITAT recruitment portal: itat.gov.in/recruitments
Register and fill in the online application form with personal, educational and category details

Applicants wishing to apply for both Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary posts need to submit only a single application - a single examination will be conducted for both posts, and the post offered will depend on the rank secured in the consolidated merit list
Upload self-attested copies of all required documents as listed above
Submit the application within 30 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in "Employment News"

Regularly check the registered email and the official ITAT website (https://www.itat.gov.in) for updates on admit cards, skill test/interview call letters and results, as all communication will be sent by email only
Applications must be submitted online only; no other mode of application is accepted as per the notification.
important_instructions: Any physical correspondence, communication or queries from individual candidates regarding the Written Examination, Skill Test, Personal Interview or final result shall NOT be entertained by the office. Admit cards, discrepancies in applications, call letters and results will be communicated to candidates through email only, at the address provided in the application; general notifications will also be uploaded on the official ITAT website. Selected candidates shall be on probation for a period of 2 years from the date of joining the post. Selected candidates shall be liable to be posted at any of the Benches of ITAT across the country. All disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of courts at Mumbai. The decision of ITAT on all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, and mode of selection, shall be final; no query or correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: ITAT Vacancy Circular PDF Download
content_sections.text: The official document is Vacancy Circular No. F.301-Ad(ATD)/DR/2026-27 issued by ITAT, Ministry of Law & Justice, Government of India, from its office at 3rd & 4th Floor, Pratishtha Bhavan, Old CGO Building, 101, Maharshi Karve Marg, Mumbai-400 020. ITAT Senior Private Secretary & Private Secretary Recruitment 2026 Employment News Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: ITAT Private Secretary Recruitment 2026-27: Quick Overview

rows.details: Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT), Ministry of Law & Justice, Government of India
Various Benches of ITAT across India

rows.date: Within 30 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in "Employment News"

content_sections.heading: ITAT Vacancy Details 2026-27
content_sections.text: The number of vacancies is liable to increase or decrease as a result of revival, augmentation or relaxation of posts by the Government of India, as per the notification.

content_sections.heading: ITAT Recruitment Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Degree of a recognized University or equivalent Speed of 120 words per minute (WPM) in English Shorthand Working knowledge of computers, with the ability to operate software such as Microsoft Office, Excel or Page-Maker These educational and other qualifications are common for both the Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary posts, as per the official notification. Nationality: applicants must be Indian nationals as defined under the Constitution of India or any law made by Parliament to regulate citizenship rights.

rows.pay_scale: ₹47,600 to ₹1,51,100 - Pay Level-8 (Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC)
₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400 - Pay Level-7 (Pay Matrix as per 7th CPC)

content_sections.heading: ITAT Selection Process 2026-27
content_sections.text: Recruitment is through a Written Examination, Skill Test, followed by a Personal Interview. The written examination and skill test will be conducted at 9 stations: Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Chennai, Bangalore, Guwahati, Lucknow, Ahmedabad and Hyderabad. Candidates must opt for two centres of choice in order of preference. If a sufficient number of candidates is not received for a particular station, ITAT has the sole discretion to cancel that centre, and the candidate will be required to appear at the second centre chosen by them. The Personal Interview carries 75 marks . Only candidates shortlisted after the Skill Test will be called for the Personal Interview; the date and venue will be notified later. The Skill Test will be evaluated only for candidates who secure the minimum qualifying marks in the Written Examination. The overall merit list will be drawn on the basis of combined merit acquired in the Skill Test and the Personal Interview. Both the Written Examination papers and the Skill Test will be conducted in English medium only. SC/ST candidates already in service of the Central/State Government, a Central/State Government Corporation, Public Sector Undertaking, Local Government Institute or Panchayat, etc., shall be reimbursed 2nd Class Ordinary/Sleeper Class railway fare or bus fare, on production of tickets as per Government of India guidelines, for appearing in the Skill Test and Personal Interview.

rows.subject: General English
rows.syllabus: Essay of minimum 250 words, Letter Writing, Precis Writing and Grammar

rows.subject: General Knowledge & Reasoning
rows.syllabus: General Knowledge/General Studies, Logical Reasoning, Current Affairs

rows.requirement: English Shorthand @120 WPM, English Typing @45 WPM

content_sections.heading: ITAT Recruitment Documents Required
content_sections.text: Matriculation certificate as proof of date of birth Graduation Degree Certificate/Graduation Mark Sheets for all semesters/years, along with Provisional Certificate Caste certificate in respect of SC/ST, issued by the Competent Authority, wherever applicable Latest Non-Creamy Layer certificate in respect of OBC, issued by the Competent Authority, wherever applicable Latest Income & Asset Certificate in respect of EWS, issued by the Competent Authority, wherever applicable Disability certificate in respect of Persons with Disability (PWD), wherever applicable, indicating nature and percentage of disability, issued by the Competent Authority No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the parent department, in the format provided with the Vacancy Circular, wherever applicable All documents must be uploaded as self-attested copies along with the online application.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for ITAT Recruitment 2026-27
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted online only ; no other mode of application is accepted as per the notification.

content_sections.heading: ITAT Recruitment Important Instructions 2026-27
content_sections.text: Any physical correspondence, communication or queries from individual candidates regarding the Written Examination, Skill Test, Personal Interview or final result shall NOT be entertained by the office. Admit cards, discrepancies in applications, call letters and results will be communicated to candidates through email only, at the address provided in the application; general notifications will also be uploaded on the official ITAT website. Selected candidates shall be on probation for a period of 2 years from the date of joining the post. Selected candidates shall be liable to be posted at any of the Benches of ITAT across the country. All disputes are subject to the jurisdiction of courts at Mumbai. The decision of ITAT on all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, and mode of selection, shall be final; no query or correspondence in this regard will be entertained.

content_sections.heading: ITAT Recruitment Disqualification Criteria
content_sections.text: Applications will be rejected if they are: Incomplete Not supported by duly self-attested copies of relevant documents in support of age and educational qualifications Not accompanied by relevant certificates issued by the Competent Authority regarding caste, non-creamy layer status, disability or EWS category, wherever applicable Submitted by applicants against whom any criminal case is pending The candidature of any candidate found resorting to malpractice or canvassing will be outrightly rejected.

faq.question: Q1. How many vacancies are there in ITAT Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: ITAT has notified 42 vacancies in total - 22 posts of Senior Private Secretary and 20 posts of Private Secretary, both Group B Gazetted.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for ITAT Private Secretary Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: Applications must be submitted online within 30 days from the date of publication of the advertisement in "Employment News." The exact calendar date is not specified in the source document.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for ITAT Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary posts?
faq.answer: The age limit for all candidates shall not exceed 35 years, with standard age relaxation applicable for Government servants and SC/ST/OBC candidates as per Central Government instructions.

faq.question: Q4. What is the pay scale for ITAT Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary?
faq.answer: Senior Private Secretary carries Pay Level-8 (₹47,600 to ₹1,51,100), and Private Secretary carries Pay Level-7 (₹44,900 to ₹1,42,400), both as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix.

faq.question: Q5. What is the selection process for ITAT Recruitment 2026-27?
faq.answer: Selection consists of a Written Examination (General English and General Knowledge & Reasoning), a Skill Test (English Shorthand @120 WPM and English Typing @45 WPM), and a Personal Interview. The Skill Test is evaluated only for candidates who clear the qualifying marks in the Written Examination, and the final merit list is based on combined Skill Test and interview marks.

faq.question: Q6. What is the required shorthand and typing speed for ITAT Private Secretary posts?
faq.answer: Candidates must have English Shorthand speed of 120 WPM and English Typing speed of 45 WPM, as tested in the Skill Test.

faq.question: Q7. Can a candidate apply for both Senior Private Secretary and Private Secretary posts?
faq.answer: Yes. Candidates wishing to apply for both posts need to submit only a single application, since a single examination is conducted for both posts, and the post offered depends on the candidate's rank in the consolidated merit list.

faq.question: Q8. What are the minimum qualifying marks in the ITAT written examination?
faq.answer: General category candidates need a minimum of 40% in each paper and 50% aggregate; OBC/EWS candidates need 35% in each paper and 45% aggregate; SC/ST/PWD candidates need 30% in each paper and 40% aggregate.

faq.question: Q9. Is there a probation period after selection to ITAT posts?
faq.answer: Yes. Selected candidates shall be on probation for a period of 2 years from the date of joining the post.

faq.question: Q10. How will ITAT communicate admit cards and results to candidates?
faq.answer: All communication regarding admit cards, discrepancies in applications, call letters for Skill Test/Personal Interview and final results will be sent by email only, to the address provided in the application. General notifications will also be uploaded on the official ITAT website, https://www.itat.gov.in.
